page: Amazon Web Services g4ad.16xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores from an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. For graphics and parallel processing workloads, the server is equipped with four AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPUs delivering a total of 32 GB of VRAM.

Local storage is provided via 2400 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 25 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong performance in Passmark CPU tests, Passmark memory tests, and Geekbench ray tracing, all scoring in the top 25%. However, single-core stress-ng performance is positioned in the bottom 10%, and multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores fall within the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth and LLM inference for small and medium models also register average performance, though text generation for large 70B LLM models is weak, placing in the bottom 25%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the g4ad.16xlarge offers a dense allocation of AMD GPUs and local NVMe storage, making it a viable option for graphics-heavy applications without the premium associated with alternative GPU architectures. Tradeoffs include lower single-core compute efficiency and limited suitability for large-scale LLM text generation. The instance is best suited for workloads such as ray tracing, graphics rendering, database operations, and moderate machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services g4ad.16xlarge is a graphics-intensive server utilizing the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores. It features 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory, four AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPUs with 32 GB of total VRAM, and 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network baseline bandwidth is 25 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates strong performance in ray tracing, Passmark CPU, and Passmark memory tests, though single-core stress-ng performance is poor, and large LLM text generation is weak. Qualitatively, the instance provides a cost-efficient profile for GPU-accelerated graphics tasks and local storage-heavy operations. It is suited for graphics rendering, ray tracing, database operations, and moderate machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g4ad.16xlarge is an AMD-powered server with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, four AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPUs, and 2400 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for graphics and ray tracing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g4ad.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, four AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPUs, and 2400 GB NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An AMD-powered server featuring four dedicated GPUs and local NVMe storage for graphics-intensive workloads and ray tracing.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor
Four AMD Radeon Pro Navi V520 GPUs with 32 GB total VRAM
2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Strong benchmark performance in ray tracing and Passmark CPU tests
Poor single-core CPU performance and weak large-model LLM text generation
Suited for graphics rendering, database operations, and moderate machine learning
